meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve Scaling Issues
content.seo_description: Learn to master project management and overcome scaling bottlenecks. A beginner guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build faster.
content.answer_block: To scale your development, shift from ad-hoc coding to a structured project management workflow. By breaking monoliths into atomic components and using AI-driven orchestration, you eliminate technical debt early. Implementing a disciplined tracking system ensures your code remains maintainable, modular, and ready for rapid iteration as your feature set grows.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a thousand lines of code, but the vision you started with is fracturing. You hit a wall: every new feature breaks three old ones, and the complexity is suffocating your progress. This isn't a failure of skill; it is the natural friction of scaling without a blueprint.

True engineering isn't just about writing logic; it's about managing the architecture of your ideas. By shifting your mindset toward structured project management, you transform your messy repository into a professional-grade engine. Let's rebuild your workflow so your code finally scales at the speed of your ambition.

steps.title: Decompose the Monolith
steps.description: Break your project into distinct, manageable domain modules to prevent dependency hell.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest a directory structure that separates concerns into atomic, decoupled modules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify top 3 areas for modularization in this repo'

steps.title: Implement Ticket-Driven Development
steps.description: Treat every feature as a discrete task to track progress and prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a task tracking template for my project that defines inputs, outputs, and acceptance criteria for new features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a project_tasks.md file and populate it with the next 5 logical steps'

steps.title: Automate Documentation Sync
steps.description: Ensure your README and technical docs reflect current code state automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a script that scans my core logic and updates the technical documentation automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Sync README.md with the current implementation details'

steps.title: Define Robust Error Boundaries
steps.description: Prevent cascading failures by implementing structured error handling at the module level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current error handling and propose a standardized wrapper pattern for all async operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Apply standardized error handling to all API service files'

steps.title: Versioned Feature Flagging
steps.description: Isolate experimental features so they don't compromise your stable build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lightweight feature flag implementation that allows me to toggle new modules on and off.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement a feature flag helper in utils/flags.ts'

pro_tips: Always commit after every single 'atomic' task completion, never mid-refactor.
Use a 'scratchpad' file for ideas that are out of scope for the current sprint.
If a module exceeds 300 lines, it is time to split it; complexity kills velocity.
Prioritize writing tests for your 'happy path' before adding complex edge case logic.
Treat your CLI commands as documentation by storing them in a 'scripts' folder.
content.next_steps: Once your project is structured, begin your first sprint by tackling the core utility module. Document your progress in your repository to build a professional portfolio that demonstrates not just code, but architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own code and start engineering your future. With these tools, you are no longer just a student, but a builder with a scalable process. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
